summary: This cluster focuses on the taxonomy, morphology, and phycocolloid potential of the red alga *Grateloupia filicina* (Halymeniaceae, Rhodophyta) along the Indian coast. Research characterized the species’ multiaxial thallus development, dioecious reproductive structures (including carpogonial branches and tetrasporangia), and the presence of two intraspecific taxa, *var. luxurians* and *var. filicina*, identified through both morphological and genetic data. A key finding across the cluster is the species’ ecological distribution across multiple Indian coastal habitats and its commercial importance as a source of carrageenan.
